title: Fix crash on app startup before authentication screen

requirements.summary: Fix the unhandled error causing app crash before login screen is shown
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app at the root URL no longer shows the error card 'Une erreur s'est produite'
The login/authentication screen (LoginPage) is displayed correctly on fresh load without any prior interaction
No unhandled exceptions are thrown during the initialization phase before authentication
The ErrorBoundary does not trigger on normal app startup
The fix works on mobile Chrome (Android) as shown in the screenshots

file_operations.description: Wrap the root React.render call in a try-catch block to catch and log any synchronous errors during app mount. Ensure service worker registration is non-blocking and failures do not prevent app initialization.

Remove the AuthenticatedOnly guard from the root RouterProvider component that redirects before Internet Identity finishes initializing. Replace with a conditional render that shows LoginPage directly while InternetIdentityProvider is still loading, avoiding premature redirects that trigger route errors.

Add defensive checks to prevent rendering errors when authentication state is undefined or still initializing. Ensure the guard does not throw errors if useInternetIdentity returns an incomplete state during the first render cycle.

Wrap all IndexedDB initialization calls (openDB, store creation) in try-catch blocks to handle cases where IndexedDB is unavailable, blocked, or fails to open. Return a fallback or no-op implementation if IndexedDB setup fails, ensuring app initialization continues gracefully.

Wrap the service worker registration in a try-catch block and log errors without rethrowing. Ensure that service worker failures (e.g., due to insecure contexts or unsupported browsers) do not propagate to the React error boundary.

requirements.summary: Add defensive error handling in app bootstrap and initialization code
acceptanceCriteria: Any error during bootstrap (AuthClient init, IndexedDB setup, service worker registration) is caught and logged without crashing the UI
The app falls back gracefully to the login screen even if a non-critical initialization step fails
The ErrorBoundary is not triggered by initialization-phase errors

file_operations.description: Wrap actor creation and AuthClient initialization in a try-catch block. If actor initialization fails, return a null actor and set an error state instead of throwing. Ensure useQuery is disabled when actor is null to prevent query execution before initialization completes.

Add error boundary fallback rendering within LoginPage component to catch and display initialization errors without blocking the login UI. Show a message if Internet Identity fails to initialize, with a retry button that reloads the page.

Update the ErrorBoundary component to detect if the error occurred during app initialization (before authentication) and render a specialized recovery message in French that directs users to reload or clear cache, rather than a generic error screen.
